qna_pairs.q: What makes MarbMarket different from other DEXs on MegaETH?
qna_pairs.a: MarbMarket is the first veDEX (Vote-Escrowed DEX) on MegaETH, using the ve(3,3) model where longer token locks give more voting power and rewards. Unlike other DEXs, it has a fair launch with no presale, no VC backing, and no team dump - all tokens go to the community from day one.

qna_pairs.q: How exactly do I earn rewards by locking MARB tokens?
qna_pairs.a: When you lock MARB tokens, you receive veMARB which gives you voting power on LP pool emissions. Longer locks = more voting power and more rewards. Projects will bribe veMARB holders for their votes, with those fees flowing back to stakers instead of being dumped.

qna_pairs.q: Is this ve(3,3) model proven to work on other chains?
qna_pairs.a: Yes! The ve(3,3) model was pioneered by Andre Cronje's Solidly and has been successful on Aerodrome (Base) and Velodrome (Optimism). These platforms have shown how the model creates a self-reinforcing cycle where fees flow back to token stakers rather than being extracted by early investors.

qna_pairs.q: What happens if a project stops bribing for votes? Will the rewards dry up?
qna_pairs.a: That's a fair question. The bribe model does rely on projects actively participating. However, the ve(3,3) structure also captures protocol fees which get distributed to veMARB holders. Over time, as trading volume grows, these protocol fees should provide a base level of rewards even if some projects reduce their bribes.

qna_pairs.q: How can I participate when it launches, and will there be any minimum requirements?
qna_pairs.a: MarbMarket is having a fair launch with no minimum requirements - anyone can start earning from day one. You'll be able to check their Twitter at x.com/Marb_market for the exact launch date and instructions on how to start locking MARB and earning veMARB rewards.

qna_pairs.q: How does the ve(3,3) model actually work for someone new to this?
qna_pairs.a: When you lock MARB tokens, you get veMARB which gives you voting power. The longer you lock, the more power you have and the more rewards you earn. Each week, veMARB holders vote on which LP pools get emissions, so projects actually bribe you for your vote.

qna_pairs.q: What's the advantage of this over regular DEXes like Uniswap?
qna_pairs.a: Unlike regular DEXes where fees go to liquidity providers and get dumped, MarbMarket's model sends fees to token stakers. This creates a self-reinforcing cycle where the token actually has value from the fees, not just speculation.

qna_pairs.q: Is this really a fair launch if there's no team tokens at all?
qna_pairs.a: That's the best part - it's a true fair launch with no presale, no VC allocation, and no team dump. All tokens go to the community from day one, which means no early investors can dump on regular users.

qna_pairs.q: How will this handle the initial liquidity problem that kills many DEXes?
qna_pairs.a: The ve(3,3) model naturally incentivizes liquidity because projects bribe veMARB holders for votes. This creates organic demand for liquidity pools without needing massive initial incentives, as seen with Aerodrome on Base.

qna_pairs.q: When exactly is the launch date and what chains will it be on?
qna_pairs.a: MarbMarket is launching on MegaETH in Q2, and it'll be the first veDEX on that chain. MegaETH is the L2 solution for Ethereum, so you'll get the security of ETH with lower fees. No other chains are planned at this time.

content: Spent last night digging into the MarbMarket docs because the ve(3,3) model on MegaETH caught my eye. It reminds me of the early Aerodrome days on Base where the tokenomics actually aligned incentives. You lock MARB for veMARB to vote on emissions, longer locks mean more power, and protocols bribe you for that vote. It creates a loop where fees flow back to stakers instead of just getting dumped. What stands out is the fair launch. No presale, no VC allocation, no team dump. The community gets the tokens from day one. I think this matters because we have seen how VC open up kill momentum on other chains. If you missed the Solidly fork on Optimism, this setup on MegaETH looks worth watching. You can check the mechanics at x.com/Marb_market. @RallyOnChain caught my attention with this one. I am curious if this model can sustain high APYs without initial liquidity. Thoughts?
